description_long: This is a classic cylindrical red postbox, reminiscent of the British Royal Mail pillar box design, with a domed cap and a slightly flared rim at the top. The body is predominantly red, with a black base that appears robust and weather-resistant. On the front, there is a rectangular mail slot set into a recessed panel, above which sits a collection times information plaque. The surface shows subtle texturing, suggesting a painted metal construction, and the overall form is solid and sturdy. No visible postal service logo or insignia is present on the exterior. The postbox is designed to be a fixed, public street fixture for mail collection.
description: A cylindrical red postbox with a domed top, black base, and a front-facing mail slot with an information plaque.
description_view_0: Front view showing the mail slot and a rectangular collection times panel on the upper front.
description_view_1: Left side view, showing the cylindrical body and the edge of the mail slot and panel.
description_view_2: Rear view, displaying the plain cylindrical back with no visible openings or panels.
description_view_3: Right side view, similar to the left, with the cylindrical body and edge of the front features visible.
attribute_mail_slot_location: centered on the front face near the top
attribute_collection_times_panel: rectangular panel directly below the mail slot on the front face
attribute_door_or_access_panel: no visible access panel; likely present on the rear or side but not shown
attribute_posting_aperture_design: horizontal rectangular slot with slightly beveled edges
